Contributors: 2018: Gustavo Correa, Dantong(Claire) Dong, Brendan Hogg, Yunfan(Daniel) Hua, Yuxiang Yang, Shuo Yang 2017: Raghav Anand, Patrick Chao, Eric Eom, Andrew Fearing, John Lin, Simin Liu, Grant Walton, Yuxiang Yang 2016 and before: Galen Sa

ant Walton, Yuxiang Yang 2016 and before: Galen Savidge, Dr. Duncan Haldane, Jeffery Kurohara Overview The OpenRoACH is a 15-cm long and 200-gram hexapod crawling robot. It is the smallest legged robot running the Robot Operating System (ROS) onboard

t running the Robot Operating System (ROS) onboard. With two Pololu metal gear DC motors and a Beagle Bone Blue board, OpenRoACH is underactuated, affordable ($150), and fully open-source. Fabrication is straightforward with bench-top fast-prototypin

is straightforward with bench-top fast-prototyping machines such as a laser cutter and a 3D printer. Assembly can be done manually with hot glue, super glue and screws by one person in two hours. Key features include: dynamical walking and running c

features include: dynamical walking and running capabilities on multiple surfaces and terrain. multi-model sensory capacity with cameras, colour tracking sensors, linescan sensors, gyroscopes, accelerometers, and Beacon sensors. durable with 24-hour

rometers, and Beacon sensors. durable with 24-hour continuous burn-in test walking on a tread mill. 200 gram payload capacity dynamically. onboard ROS, optional mbed microcontroller The present complete version for mechanical design is version 1.2. P

te version for mechanical design is version 1.2. Publication and Video Wang, L., Yang, Y., Correa, G., Karydis, K., and Fearing, R. S. (2019) OpenRoACH: A durable open-source hexapedal platform with onboard Robot Operating System (ROS). In Proceeding

nboard Robot Operating System (ROS). In Proceedings of the 2019 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA), 20-24 May 2019, Montreal, Canada. In press. PrePrint A video of OpenRoACH Mechanical Design and Fabrication Present versi

CH Mechanical Design and Fabrication Present version (v1.2) CAD files Fabrication and Assembly Instructions Sensors Line scanner sensor Line Scanner Freescale linescan sensor reading app Proximity sensor and electromagnet Light Sensor and Electromagn

sor and electromagnet Light Sensor and Electromagnet Electronics and Microcontrollers PCB carrier board Locomotion Control Color Object following control based on Pixy Sensor----100% CAMERA (If you have any question, E-mail me: YangShuo960502 at foxm

ve any question, E-mail me: YangShuo960502 at foxmail.com) Line following control based on a Freescale linescan sensor Direction Control based on a pair of Pololu beacons(Final Updated) Line following based on Freescale linescan sensor and accelerame

based on Freescale linescan sensor and accelerameter Ros Integration See https://github.com/yxyang/ros_openroach for details of setup. Cost of Parts Link to the Document Version history v1.2: September 2017-September 2018 ROS enabled with Beagle Bon

er 2017-September 2018 ROS enabled with Beagle Bone Blue, reduced electronics mass to 40 g. Hip flexure shape modified. Side shaft-tightening-screw hole added to pulley-flange. v1.1: July 2017-August 2017 Chassis middle lowered to accommodate electro

2017 Chassis middle lowered to accommodate electronics. Tested higher speed motors, 50:1 Micro Metal Gearmotor HP . v1.1 CAD files v1.0: June 2017-July 2017 Two-piece 3D-printable pulleys. A 3D-printable motor mounting base. Reuse the initial motor d

ble motor mounting base. Reuse the initial motor driver on Zumy board, remove many wiring cables. Legs from C-shape to O-shape. v0.5: February 2017-May 2017 Chassis made from poster board, nylon and PET. Dual shafts, 298:1 Micro Metal Gearmotor HP ,

ET. Dual shafts, 298:1 Micro Metal Gearmotor HP , three-piece 3D-printable pulleys, timing belts, and a laser cut motor mounting base. Self-contained with 22-gram battery. v0.3: November 2016 - January 2017 Chassis made from PET and nylon. Leg transm

y 2017 Chassis made from PET and nylon. Leg transmission bar made from Delrin. Self-contained with onboard Zumy board, Dual MC33926 Motor Driver Carrier , 50-gram battery, and line scanner sensor. Realized path following with ∞ trajectory three times

lized path following with ∞ trajectory three times. v0.1: Before October 2016 A 1.5x scaled VelociRoACH chassis made from poster board and nylon with a Tamiya twin motor gearbox . AutoCAD drawings, assembly guide, and bill of materials for v0.1 are a
